Swing Profiles & Measurements

Real swing frames from the dataset. Faces blurred. Each swing type shown with its top scorer.

Swing Profiles — Best Scorer Per Type
POSTER — Full Lateral Shift + Lead Leg Post
The most powerful delivery pattern. 8 of 9 FO pros.
FO Good Pattern Tiger FO
P1
P5
P7
Full weight shift toward target. Lead leg straightens at impact creating a stable post — the pivot point that converts lateral momentum into rotational speed.
ShiftAmp 0.66 · SpineTilt 4.2° · LLET 6.95 · CompHold 1.95
A-FRAME — Rotation Dominant
Power through rotation, not lateral shift.
FO Good Pattern Maria Fasi FO
P1
P5
P7
Less lateral shift, more rotational power. Both legs form an A-shape at impact. Power comes from spine tilt, hip clearing, compression and lag.
ShiftAmp 0.38 · SpineTilt 3.9° · Compression 0.451 · Lag@P7 31°
SHALLOWER — Standard Pro Delivery
On-plane takeaway → inside delivery. The textbook pattern.
DTL Good Pattern Tiger DTL
P4
P6
P7
Club stays on plane during backswing, then shallows in transition to deliver from slightly inside. The gold standard.
TakeawayPath 0° · ClubPath +6° · Shallowing ∆ +6° · ShoDrop -0.147
LOOPER — Outside Takeaway + Reroute
Figure-8 path. Unconventional but elite.
DTL Good Pattern Matt Wolff DTL
P3
P5
P7
Club goes outside early, then reroutes dramatically in transition — dropping under the plane for a strong inside delivery.
TakeawayPath +3.4° · ClubPath +12° · Shallowing ∆ +8.6° · ShoDrop -0.168
SLOTTER — Elite Hand Drop
Tight wrist action + strong shoulder drive.
DTL Good Pattern Colin Morikawa DTL
P4
P5
P7
Hands drop straight into the slot. Combined with strong shoulder drive, produces the tightest, most efficient delivery.
WristDrop 0.192 · ShoDrop -0.169 · ClubPath +3° · ShaftLean 1.02
ONE-PLANE — Minimal Change
Compact, consistent. Same plane throughout.
DTL Good Pattern DJ Wood DTL
P3
P5
P7
Club stays on the same plane from takeaway through delivery. Compact, repeatable, consistent.
TakeawayPath +0.5° · ClubPath -2° · Shallowing ∆ -2.5° · ShoDrop -0.253
Key Measurements — Pro Reference (Tiger & Rory)
Weight Shift — Posting Up
Rory FO
At impact the lead leg is straight (posted), hips have shifted past center, and spine is tilted away. This converts lateral momentum into rotational speed.
Kinematic Chain — Sequence
Tiger FO
The power sequence: hips lead → torso follows → arms trail → club last. Each segment accelerates the next.
Compression Hold
Tiger FO
The hip drop is loaded and maintained as the lateral shift begins. Tiger holds for 2.0 P-frames — the spring stays coiled while the body moves.
Shoulder Drive — Down and Through
Rory DTL
The trail shoulder drives down through impact — working under the chin. Creates shaft lean, compresses the ball, maintains the low point.
